description: You are a Business Intelligence Analyst at a global hospitality group. Using the REVENUE_ANALYST schema (revenue_analyst/RevMgmt2024 on XEPDB1), cleanse the historical hotel booking data and build analytical views to optimize the dynamic overbooking engine. Create and execute a PL/SQL procedure named PROC_CLEANSE_BOOKINGS that removes records from the HOTEL_BOOKINGS table where the total guest count (adults + children + babies) is zero or the Average Daily Rate (adr) is negative. Develop a view named CANCELLATION_CUBE_VW using the CUBE extension to group by hotel, market_segment, and deposit_type, calculating total_bookings, cancellations (sum of is_canceled), and cancellation_rate (rounded to 4 decimal places). Create a view named ADR_PERCENTILES_VW to calculate the median ADR using the PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) function grouped by hotel, reserved_room_type, and is_canceled. Construct a view named LOST_REVENUE_PIVOT_VW that calculates total lost revenue (adr * (stays_in_weekend_nights + stays_in_week_nights)) from canceled bookings (is_canceled = 1), using the PIVOT operator to transform deposit_type values into columns aliased as no_deposit, non_refund, and refundable, grouped by hotel and arrival_date_year. Export the contents of LOST_REVENUE_PIVOT_VW to /home/ga/Documents/exports/lost_revenue.csv.
